facts.text: Melbourne hosted the 1956 Summer Olympics, the first Games held outside Europe and the United States.
Flinders Street station was the world's busiest passenger station in 1927, and Melbourne's tram network became the largest in the world by the 1940s.
State Library Victoria, founded in 1854, is one of the world's oldest free public libraries and was the fourth most‑visited library globally in 2018.
Melbourne was founded in 1835 by free settlers from Van Diemen's Land, and the city was named after British Prime Minister William Lamb, 2nd Viscount Melbourne, in 1837.
The Heidelberg School, an Australian impressionist movement, originated in Melbourne where artists camped en plein air in the suburb of Heidelberg during the 1880s.
Docklands Studios Melbourne, the city's largest film and television studio complex, has attracted major international productions such as "The Great Gatsby" and "The Hobbit" series.
